  Hi, Dear. I’m a Senior Software Engineer / Full-Stack Developer with 9+ years of experience building scalable, high-performance systems across a wide range of industries.
  
  My core expertise lies in backend engineering, where I specialize in C++, PHP, Go, Rust, Python (Flask/Django), Java, and C# .NET. I’ve designed and deployed distributed systems using PostgreSQL, NoSQL, AWS, Docker, and CI/CD pipelines leveraging Kubernetes and Kafka.
  
  On the frontend, I bring strong experience with React.js, Next.js, Angular, Vue.js, as well as cross-platform mobile development using Kotlin, Swift, Flutter and React Native.
  
  Over the years, I’ve delivered impactful solutions including:

  I enjoy solving complex technical challenges, optimizing system performance, and building reliable, scalable products that deliver real business value.

  [Clipboard]
  Two years ago I turned down a project. Best technical decision I made all year — and I didn't write a single line of code to make it.
  
  The founder wanted to rebuild his entire platform from scratch because it "felt slow." I spent an afternoon in the codebase and found the real issue: three database queries and one missing cache. Two days of work, not a six-month rewrite.
  
  He didn't need a new platform. He needed someone honest enough to tell him he didn't.
  
  That's the part of this job I've come to value most in 9+ years. The best engineers aren't the ones who say yes to every rebuild, every framework migration, every shiny idea. They're the ones who protect your time and money by finding the smallest change that actually solves the problem.
  
  That instinct is sharpened by working where the cost of getting it wrong is real:
  
  → Trading systems (DeFi, DEX) where a bad call is measured in dollars, not bug reports
  → Computer-vision video pipelines (OpenCV, FFmpeg, YOLO) where the wrong approach quietly burns your entire compute budget
  → Payments and healthcare, where "good enough" isn't
  
  The depth is there when it's genuinely needed — C++, Go, Rust, Python, Java, C#/.NET, React, Next.js, AWS, Kubernetes. But my most valuable skill is knowing when the answer is "we don't need that."
  
  I'm looking to join one ambitious team full-time as a senior engineer — partnering with a founder, CEO, or CTO building something real, across the US, Germany, the Netherlands, Cyprus, or Europe.
  
  Founders: what's the most expensive rebuild you're glad you DIDN'T do? 👇
